scene_structure.concept: One continuous descending track — camera follows the ball from top to bottom. Each song = new section with its own rail shape and instrument type. The transition between sections is visual: new rails appear below as old ones exit the top of frame.
scene_structure.loop: End of track connects back to start — seamless repeat

environment.wall_note: NOT flat — warm amber glow pools from right side, gradient visible. Use area light + subtle gradient texture.

light_accent.note: Visible in Hide (Dark Fantasy) — red/magenta glow cast on xylophone surface by ball

camera.note: NOT perpendicular — slight oblique angle, creates depth. Rotates ~4° on Z axis.
camera.motion: follows ball continuously, smooth damp, slight lag

ball.shadow: soft drop shadow on wall behind

count_rules.multi_ball_rule: additional balls are SLIGHTLY behind on same track, offset by one note

shapes_per_section.steel_pan: smooth arc / S-curve, concave cradle for the ball
shapes_per_section.confort_chain: straight horizontal segments connected by small curves, chain-like pattern
shapes_per_section.fallen_down: NO rails — instruments suspended by thin vertical wires from above
shapes_per_section.hide_dark_fantasy: flat wide platform grid (like a xylophone), multiple parallel bars

pitch_mapping.rule: MIDI pitch → instrument size. Higher pitch = smaller instrument. Laid out left-to-right or top-to-bottom by pitch order.
pitch_mapping.velocity_to_scale: high velocity → slightly larger hit visual (squash/stretch on impact)

text_overlay.implementation: Blender compositor text node or FFmpeg drawtext

animation.ball_speed: constant except on impacts — slight deceleration on hit, acceleration away
